notes: Confirmed across all tested versions (2.80.2, 3.119.2, main): SKVertices.CreateCopy only accepts array parameters with no ReadOnlySpan<T> overloads, no explicit vertex/index count parameters, and no SKVerticesBuilder type. The underlying C API (sk_vertices_make_copy) already accepts explicit vertexCount/indexCount but the C# wrapper derives these from array Length. SkVertices::Builder exists in upstream C++ Skia but has no C API shim. Users must allocate sub-arrays to pass subsets.

reproductionSteps.description: Verify C API header (sk_vertices.h) and C# source (SKVertices.cs) — C API accepts explicit vertexCount/indexCount but C# derives from array Length; no builder functions in C API
reproductionSteps.output:
sk_vertices.h: sk_vertices_make_copy(vmode, vertexCount, positions, texs, colors, indexCount, indices) — accepts explicit counts.
SKVertices.cs: CreateCopy derives vertexCount from positions.Length and indexCount from indices?.Length. No Span overloads. No Builder class. C API has no sk_vertices_builder_* functions.

versionResults.notes: Reflection confirms no Span overloads, no Builder type. Native call failed due to missing arm64 binary (expected for this old version) but API surface inspection completed.
Full run succeeded. No Span overloads, no Builder type. Demonstrated forced array allocation when using subset of shared array.
Built from main branch source. Same API gap — no Span overloads, no Builder type. C API and C# source both confirmed unchanged.

actionability.reason: Well-specified enhancement with clear implementation path. Maintainer expressed preference for ReadOnlySpan<T> approach in closed PR #2432. The C API already supports explicit counts; the gap is in the C# wrapper and missing Builder C API shim.

actions.description: Cross-reference #2431 (same offset/count request) and closed PR #2432

proposedResponse.body:
Confirmed that the SKVertices API gap described in this issue still exists as of SkiaSharp 3.119.2 and the current main branch.

**Findings:**
- `SKVertices.CreateCopy` only accepts `SKPoint[]`, `SKColor[]`, and `UInt16[]` parameters — no `ReadOnlySpan<T>` overloads
- The C# wrapper derives `vertexCount` from `positions.Length` and `indexCount` from `indices.Length`, hiding the explicit count parameters that the underlying C API (`sk_vertices_make_copy`) already accepts
- No `SKVerticesBuilder` class exists — `SkVertices::Builder` in upstream Skia has no C API shim
- Users must allocate sub-arrays to use a subset of a shared buffer, incurring unnecessary GC pressure

**Workaround:** Use `Array.Copy` or `array.AsSpan(offset, count).ToArray()` to create sub-arrays before passing to `CreateCopy`.

This overlaps with #2431, and a related PR (#2432) was closed with maintainer feedback preferring a `ReadOnlySpan<T>`-based approach.

workarounds: Use Array.Copy or array.AsSpan(offset, count).ToArray() to create sub-arrays before passing to CreateCopy
